# Transactions and Travel Rule settings
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/transactions-and-travel-rule-settings

The transactions and Travel Rule settings allow you to adjust the transaction monitoring flow to your business specifics and regulatory requirements. 

Under Transaction Monitoring → Settings, you can configure the following:

- General  — lets you adjust basic Transaction Monitoring settings, as described in this section.
- Tags — lets you create and manage tags that can be used for applicant risk scoring and other purposes, as described in this section.
- AML Screening — lets you select the provider for AML watchlist checks and customize the search configuration for screening, as described in this section.

 If your organization has the Travel Rule service enabled, you will also see two additional menu items:

- Travel Rule — lets you configure your Travel Rule workflows to meet global and local regulatory requirements. For example, you can set confirmation timeouts for outgoing Travel Rule messages or specify which data will be shared with counterparty VASPs. For incoming transactions, you can configure wallet ownership verification and define how data comparison should be performed. The detailed guidance on configuring Travel Rule is provided in this article. 
- Integration Wizard — lets you streamline your onboarding to Sumsub’s Travel Rule solution. You can complete a guided questionnaire to share your organization’s needs, and we will generate a tailored step-by-step integration guide with relevant documentation links. The Integration Wizard is described in this article.

# General transactions and Travel Rule settings

In the General settings, you can configure the following:

# Transactions and Travel Rule tags settings

In the Tags settings, you can manage predefined tags and create new ones.

To edit a predefined tag:

1. In the Transactions and Travel Rule Settings, navigate to Tags.
2. Click on the pencil icon next to the tag.
3. Change the tag bar color or weight. Enable (or disable) the Score applicant checkbox. To learn more about setting up tags, refer to this article.
4. Save your changes.

To create a new tag:

1. In the Transactions and Travel Rule Settings, navigate to Tags.
2. Enter the tag name, choose the bar color, select the Score applicant checkbox if you want to use the tag for scoring applicants as part of ongoing risk assessment, and set its weight in %.
3. Save your changes.

# Transactions and Travel Rule AML Screening settings

To configure transaction counterparty AML screening:

1. In the Transactions and Travel Rule Settings, navigate to AML Screening.
2. Select a provider that you want to use for AML screening. If the provider is locked, enter your credentials, as described in ComplyAdvantage and World-Check One.
3. Select a search configuration. You can search by the warning types or by providing a search profile identifier:
   - When searching by the warning types, specify the categories of PEPs for which you want to screen your applicants and add the associated types of adverse media.
   - When searching by custom search profiles, enter a profile identifier. To search for profiles from your Comply Advantage key, make sure to provide the key, as described in this article.
4. Configure name-matching criteria and desired fuzzy-matching levels:
   - Strict matching — to flag applicants only upon an exact match.
   - Default matching — to allow for variations in spelling.
   - Fuzzy matching — to allow for variations in the range of names.
5. Set the expiration period for AML cases in days. A new case will be created automatically if, at the time of transaction creation, the latest case is older than the selected number of days.
6. Save your changes.

# Transaction Monitoring analytics
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/transaction-monitoring-analytics

The Analytics section helps you monitor Transaction Monitoring and Travel Rule activity through dedicated Dashboard charts. You can use it to:

- Review case outcomes.
- Compare automatic and manual review activity.
- Track transaction volume.
- Evaluate rule performance over a selected period.

In the Analytics section, you can see a number of default charts. To meet your requirements, you may also request a custom chart via one of the following methods:

- Contact your customer success manager.
- Use the chat in our Dashboard.
- Email us at business-support@sumsub.com.

# Get started with analytics

In the Dashboard, go to the Transactions and travel rule section and open Analytics.

Most charts include the same filters and control options:

- Period — use Period filter to select the date range for the widget. You can select one of the built-in options (for instance, today or last 30 days) or set up a custom period.
- Show — Time-based widgets include the Show option. Use it to define how the chart groups data within the selected period.
- Refresh — click the Refresh icon to reload chart data.
- Download CSV — click Download CSV to export chart data for additional analysis or reporting.

> 📘 Note
> 
> Charts in this section display summary data. You cannot open the related transaction, applicant, or counterparty by clicking a chart point.

# Default analytics charts

By default, the Analytics section displays the charts listed in the table below to help you monitor Transaction Monitoring and Travel Rule activity.

# View specific transaction analytics
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/view-specific-transaction-analytics

Transaction analytics help you get a bird's-eye view of your applicants’ financial activity and make informed decisions regarding questionable transactions.

# View applicants transaction analytics

To view analytical information about your applicants' transactions:

1. In the Dashboard, open the Transactions page and select any transaction. 
2. Open the Analytics tab to see the full transaction history for the selected user and specify the period to display the analytical data.

For financial transactions, you can review such information as: Transactions volume, Transactions count, Transactions count by status, and the Transactions volume for the specified period as a graph. 

The information should be interpreted in the following manner:

| Data type                    | Description                                                                     |
| :--------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Transactions volume          | The amount of money sent, received, or withdrawn by the user.                   |
| Transactions count           | The number of transactions performed within the specified period.               |
| Transactions count by status | The number of approved, declined, or suspended transactions split by direction. |

> 📘 Note
> 
> All the sums are displayed in the default currency specified in the General settings.

# Overview
URL: https://docs.sumsub.com/docs/travel-rule-overview

The rise of cryptocurrencies has brought an evolution in financial regulations to mitigate the risks associated with the sector. One such measure is the FATF Travel Rule.

Initially, the Travel Rule only applied to financial institutions; however, in 2019, the FATF expanded its recommendations to include >. 

# Key requirements

For VA transfers under the 1,000 USD/EUR threshold, FATF recommends to collect the following information:

- The name of the originator (sender) and the beneficiary (recipient).
- The VA wallet address for each or a unique transaction reference number.

For transfers exceeding the above threshold, VASPs should collect:

- The originator's name.
- The originator account number for the account used to process the transaction (for example, wallet address).
- The originator's physical (geographical) address, national identity number, and customer identification number (that is, not a transaction number) that uniquely identifies the originator to the ordering institution, or date and place of birth.
- The beneficiary's name.
- The beneficiary account number for the account used to process the transaction (for example, wallet address).

# Sumsub Travel Rule solution

Sumsub Travel Rule checks are part of our Transaction Monitoring solution. Each check includes a multi-level risk evaluation system that helps businesses determine the risk of the counterparty, and hence decide on transacting with it.

The main benefits of using the solution are:

- Seamless integration with your system and other Sumsub products. You can use the Travel Rule check along with other user verification and transaction monitoring features and AML checks to get detailed information about each transaction.
- Most popular data exchange protocols under the hood, including TRP, GTR, Sumsub custom protocol, and email notifications, to streamline the Travel Rule data exchange. The system will automatically select the quickest and easiest way to obtain the counterparty data.
- VASP attribution allowing to determine whether the VA transaction is carried out with a VASP or an unhosted wallet and establish the counterparty VASP identity.
- Screening of VA transaction participants against watchlists, global sanctions lists (OFAC, UN, HMT, EU, DFT, and so on), and adverse media.
- Secure ecosystem. Access to the most recent verification data and other documentation related to other Travel Rule ecosystem members to ensure quick confirmation of all transactions.
- Straightforward solution for the interoperability and the Sunrise issues. 
- Vast functionality that allows you to configure the exact fields when exchanging data to meet specific regulations.
- 8000+ supported virtual assets.
- Crypto wallet scoring mechanism that lets our clients set up automated transaction rejection based on the wallet status.

# Start your Travel Rule journey

[block:html]
{
  "html": "\n    .box5 {\n        background: #ffffff;\n        border: 1px solid #e1e5ea;\n        border-radius: 12px;\n        align-items: top;\n        display: grid;\n        text-decoration: Shall come into force on July 1, 2026.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulation\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Rules 2025\n                                                Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Act 2006 (AML/CTF Act)\n                                                The travel rule guidance\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulatory body\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Australian Transaction Reports and Analysis Centre (AUSTRAC).\n                                            \n                                        \n                                                                        \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No threshold. The Travel Rule applies to all virtual asset transfers irrespective of value.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n identifier; iii)\n                                                    identification number (given&nbsp;by the ordering institution); (iv) date and place of birth.\n wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                                            \n transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n       \n                                        \n                                            Domestic vs cross-border transfers\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Domestic transfers\n                                            Individual Person\n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                \n                                                    Wallet address or account number (or a unique transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                           Wallet address or account number (or a unique transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Legal Entity\n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                \n                                                    Wallet address or account number (or a unique transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                  Originator:\n                                            \n                                                Full name;\n                                                \n                                                    One of the following: i) residential address (not being a post office box); ii) unique identifier; iii)\n                                                    identification number (given&nbsp;by the ordering institution); (iv) date and place of birth.\n                                                \n                                                \n                                                    Wallet address or account number (or a unique transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                Full name;\n                                                \n                                                    Wallet address or account number (or a unique transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Legal Entity\n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                Registered name;.\n                                                \n                                                    One of the following: i) business address (not being a post office box); ii) unique identifier; iii)\n                                                    identification number (given&nbsp;by the ordering institution).\n                                                \n                                                \n                                                    Wallet address or account number (or a unique transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                Registered name;\n                                                \n                                                    Wallet address or account number (or a unique transaction reference number where no wallet or account number\n                                                    exists).\n                                                \n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Batch file transfer\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            In the event that several individual cross-border transfers from a single originator are bundled in a batch file for transmission to beneficiaries, the batch file shall contain required and accurate originator information, and full beneficiary information.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Unhosted wallet verification\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Required.\n                                            VASP must establish, in their AML/CTF policy, how they will identify the originator or beneficiary in cases where a self-hosted wallet is used and any steps they will take to verify the person who controls such a wallet. The policy must also describe how the VASP will manage and mitigate the ML/TF risk where the self-hosted wallet controller is unverified.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            VASP Due Diligence\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Required.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Time and way to share the Information\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            The regulation does not establish specific provisions. In accordance with the FATF's recommendations, the required information must be transmitted immediately and securely.\n                                        \n                                           \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Record retention\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Seven years.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                \n            \n\n            \n            Main actions required\n            \n                \n                    Obligations of the originating VASP\n                    The originating VASP must:\n                    \n                        \n                            obtain information about the originator and beneficiary from a client who initiated a virtual asset transfer;\n                        \n                        \n                            in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator\n                            and beneficiary;\n                        \n                        \n                            verify information about the originator by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        \n                        \n                            carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet – custodial or self-hosted – is used on the other\n                            end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet;\n                        \n                        conduct VASP Due Diligence;\n                        verify a self-hosted wallet to which the virtual assets should be sent (if applicable);\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary VASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the beneficiary VASP\n                    The beneficiary VASP must:\n                    \n                        \n                            carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet – custodial or self-hosted – is used on the other\n                            end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet (if necessary);\n                        \n                        conduct VASP Due Diligence;\n                        verify a self-hosted wallet from which the virtual assets were received (if applicable);\n                        \n                            receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the originating or intermediary VASP (or obtain it\n                            directly from its customer if virtual assets are received from a self-hosted wallet) and ensure that the\n                            received information is complete by comparing it with the applicable requirements;\n                        \n                        \n                            in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator\n                            and beneficiary;\n                        \n                        \n                            verify information about the beneficiary by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        \n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the intermediary VASP\n                    The intermediary VASP must:\n                    \n                        \n                            receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the previous VASP in the transfer chain and ensure\n                            that the received information is complete by comparing it with the applicable requirements;\n                        \n                        \n                            in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator\n                            and beneficiary;\n                        \n                        \n                            carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet – custodial or self-hosted – is used on the other\n                            end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet;\n                        \n                        conduct VASP Due Diligence;\n                        \n                            verify a self-hosted wallet from which the virtual assets were received or to which they should be sent (if\n                            applicable);\n                        \n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary VASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                \n            \n\n        \n    \n\n    \n        \n        \n            \n            General information\n            \n                \n                \n                    \n                        \n                            \n                                \n                                    \n                                        Subject\n                                    \n                                    \n                                        Details\n                                    \n                                \n                                \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Status\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            In force from 23 February 2022.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulation\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Regulation (EU) 2023/1113\n                                                Travel rule Guidelines\n                                                FMA Circular 04/2022\n                                                FM-GwG – Finanzmarkt-Geldwäschegesetz\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulatory body\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Österreichische Finanzmarktaufsichtsbehörde (FMA)\n                                            \n                                        \n                                                                        \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            None. The Travel Rule applies to all crypto-asset transfers irrespective of value.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Data to be shared\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Natural Person\n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                a) Full official name;\n                                                \n                                                    b) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number;\n                                                \n                                                c) Address and identification information: i) habitual residence, including country name; ii) official personal document number; iii) customer identification number, or date and place of birth.\n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                d) Full official name;\n                                                \n                                                    e) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number.\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Legal Entity\n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                f) Registered name;\n                                                \n                                                    g) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number;\n                                                \n                                                h) Registered office address;\n                                                \n                                                    i) Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) or equivalent identifier. The alternative identifier must: i) be a unique identification code for the legal entity; ii) be published in public registries; iii) be automatically issued upon entity\n                                                    formation by a public authority; iv) allow identification of name and address elements; v) be accompanied by a description of the identifier type in the messaging system.\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                j) Registered name;\n                                                \n                                                    k) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number;\n                                                \n                                                \n                                                    l) Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) or equivalent identifier. The alternative identifier must: i) be a unique identification code for the legal entity; ii) be published in public registries; iii) be automatically issued upon entity\n                                                    formation by a public authority; iv) allow identification of name and address elements; v) be accompanied by a description of the identifier type in the messaging system.\n                                                \n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                \n            \n\n            \n            Specific requirements\n            \n                \n                \n                    \n                        \n                            \n                                \n                                    \n                                        Subject\n                                    \n                                    \n                                        Details\n                                    \n                                \n                                \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold-based data\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            —\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Domestic vs cross-border transfers\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No difference.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Batch file transfer\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            In the case of a batch file transfer of crypto-assets from a single originator, the obligation on the CASP of the originator to ensure that transfers of crypto-assets are accompanied by the required information shall not apply to the individual transfers bundled together therein, provided that the batch file contains the information on the originator and beneficiary, that that information has been verified, and that the individual transfers carry the distributed ledger address of the originator where this is applicable, the crypto-asset account number of the originator where this is applicable, or the unique transaction identifier where this is applicable.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Unhosted wallet verification\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Required for transactions exceeding EUR 1,000.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            VASP Due Diligence\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            When establishing a correspondent relationship with a CASP registered outside the EU.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Time and way to share the Information\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            The required information shall be transmitted immediately and in a secure manner, and no later than the initiation of the blockchain transaction.\n                                        \n                                           \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Record retention\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            10 years.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                \n            \n\n            \n            Main actions required\n            \n                \n                    Obligations of the originating CASP\n                    The originating CASP must:\n                    \n                        obtain information about the originator and beneficiary from a client who initiated a crypto-asset transfer;\n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        verify information about the originator by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or self-hosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which CASP controls the wallet;\n                        conduct VASP Due Diligence (if applicable);\n                        verify a self-hosted wallet to which the crypto-assets should be sent (if applicable);\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary CASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the beneficiary CASP\n                    The beneficiary CASP must:\n                    \n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or self-hosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which CASP controls the wallet (if necessary);\n                        conduct VASP Due Diligence (if applicable);\n                        verify a self-hosted wallet from which the crypto-assets were received (if applicable);\n                        \n                            receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the originating or intermediary VASP (or obtain it directly from its customer if assets are received from a self-hosted wallet) and ensure that the received information\n                            is complete by comparing it with the requirements in the originating CASP's jurisdiction;\n                        \n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        verify information about the beneficiary by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the intermediary CASP\n                    The intermediary CASP must:\n                    \n                        receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the previous CASP in the chain and ensure that the received information is complete by comparing it with the requirements in the originating VASP's jurisdiction;\n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or self-hosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which CASP controls the wallet;\n                        conduct VASP Due Diligence (if applicable);\n                        verify a self-hosted wallet from which the crypto-assets were received or to which they should be sent (if applicable);\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary CASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                                        \n                \n            \n\n        \n    \n\n    \n        \n        \n            \n            General information\n            \n                \n                \n                    \n                        \n                            \n                                \n                                    \n                                        Subject\n                                    \n                                    \n                                        Details\n                                    \n                                \n                                \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Status\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            In force from December 14, 2020.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulation\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                DARE Act 2020 (Schedules 4 and 5 to the DARE Act)\n                                                Financial Transactions and Reporting Act, 2018\n                                                Financial Transactions and Reporting (Wire Transfers) Regulations, 2018\n                                                \n                                                Digital Assets and Registered Exchanges Act, 2020\n                                                Digital Assets and Registered Exchanges (Anti-money Laundering, Countering Financing of Terrorism and Countering Financing of Proliferation) Rules, 2022\n                                                \n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulatory body\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Securities Commission of the Bahamas\n                                            \n                                        \n                                                                        \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No threshold. The Travel Rule applies to all virtual asset transfers irrespective of value.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Data to be shared\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                a) Full name;\n                                                b) Account number (or a unique transaction identifier); and\n                                                c) Address or date and place of birth or the payer's national identity number or customer identification number.\n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                d) Full name;\n                                                e) Account number (or a unique transaction identifier).\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                \n            \n\n            \n            Specific requirements\n            \n                \n                \n                    \n                        \n                            \n                                \n                                    \n                                        Subject\n                                    \n                                    \n                                        Details\n                                    \n                                \n                                \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold-based data\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            —\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Domestic vs cross-border transfers\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            There is a difference.\n                                            Domestic. The following data needs to be transferred: b), but other data should be provided within three business days after request.\n                                            \n                                            Cross-border. The following data should be transferred:\n                                            \n                                                &lt; BSD 1,000: a), b), d), e);\n                                                &gt; BSD 1,000: a), b), c), d), e).\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Batch file transfer\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Where a batch file transfer comprises individual wire transfers (VA transfers) of one thousand dollars or more from a single payer (originator) to payees (beneficiary) outside The Bahamas, the\n                                                originating financial institution shall be exempted from the requirements of paragraph 3 of the Section \"The main actions required\" in respect of the inclusion of the payer's information with\n                                                each individual transfer, provided that\n                                            \n                                            \n                                                (a) the batch file contains the information required under paragraph 3 specified in Section \"The main actions required\" on\n                                                \n                                                    (i) the payer;\n                                                    (ii) the payees; and\n                                                \n                                                (b) the individual wire transfers include the payer's account number or, if no account is used , a unique transaction identifier.\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Unhosted wallet verification\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No requirement. May be applied based on risk assessment.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            VASP Due Diligence\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No requirement.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Time and way to share the Information\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            The regulation does not establish specific provisions. In accordance with the FATF's recommendations, the required information must be transmitted immediately and securely.\n                                        \n                                           \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Record retention\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            5 years.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                \n            \n\n            \n            Main actions required\n            \n                \n                    Obligations of the originating VASP\n                    The originating VASP must:\n                    \n                        obtain information about the originator and beneficiary from a client who initiated a virtual asset transfer;\n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        verify information about the originator by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or unhosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet;\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary VASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the beneficiary VASP\n                    The beneficiary VASP must:\n                    \n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or unhosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet (if necessary);\n                        \n                            receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the originating or intermediary VASP (or obtain it directly from its customer if assets are received from an unhosted wallet) and ensure that the received information is\n                            complete by comparing it with the requirements in the originating VASP's jurisdiction;\n                        \n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        verify information about the beneficiary by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the intermediary VASP\n                    The intermediary VASP must:\n                    \n                        receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the previous VASP in the chain and ensure that the received information is complete by comparing it with the requirements in the originating VASP's jurisdiction;\n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or unhosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet;\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary VASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                                        \n                \n            \n\n        \n    \n\n    \n        \n        \n            \n            General information\n                             Subject\n                                    \n                                    \n                                        Details\n                                    \n                                  Regulation\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Central Bank of Bahrain Volume 4 - Investment Business - Part A - Business Standards - FC Financial Crime - FC-11 Crypto-assets\n                                                Central Bank of Bahrain Volume 6 - Capital Markets - Part A - Market Standards - AML - Anti-Money Laundering & Combating Financial Crime - AML-2 AML/CFT Systems and Controls - AML-2A: Money Transfers and Accepted Crypto-asset Transfers\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulatory body\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Central Bank of Bahrain\n                                            \n                                        \n                                                                        \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No threshold. The Travel Rule applies to all virtual asset transfers irrespective of value.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Data to be shared\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                a) Full name;\n                                                b) Account number (e.g. IBAN or crypto-asset wallet) where such an account is used to process the transaction;\n                                                c) Address, or national identity number, or customer identification number, or date and place of birth.\n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                d) Full name; and\n                                                e) Account number (e.g. IBAN or crypto-asset wallet) where such an account is used to process the transaction.\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                \n            \n\n            \n            Specific requirements\n            \n                \n                \n                    \n                        \n                            \n                                \n                                    \n                                        Subject\n                                    \n                                    \n                                        Details\n                                    \n                                \n                                \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold-based data\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            —\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Domestic vs cross-border transfers\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No difference. VASPs must consider all transfers of crypto-assets as cross-border wire transfers rather than domestic transfers.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Batch file transfer\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            There are no special provisions.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Unhosted wallet verification\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No requirement. May be applied based on risk assessment.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            VASP Due Diligence\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No requirement.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Time and way to share the Information\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            The required information shall be transmitted immediately and in a secure manner.\n                                        \n                                           \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Record retention\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            5 years.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                \n            \n\n            \n            Main actions required\n            \n                \n                    Obligations of the originating VASP\n                    The originating VASP must:\n                    \n                        obtain information about the originator and beneficiary from a client who initiated a virtual asset transfer;\n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        verify information about the originator by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or unhosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet;\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary VASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the beneficiary VASP\n                    The beneficiary VASP must:\n                    \n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or unhosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet (if necessary);\n                        \n                            receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the originating or intermediary VASP (or obtain it directly from its customer if assets are received from an unhosted wallet) and ensure that the received information is\n                            complete by comparing it with the requirements in the originating VASP's jurisdiction;\n                        \n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        verify information about the beneficiary by comparing it with the information gathered during the KYC or KYB;\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                    \n                    Obligations of the intermediary VASP\n                    The intermediary VASP must:\n                    \n                        receive information about the originator and beneficiary from the previous VASP in the chain and ensure that the received information is complete by comparing it with the requirements in the originating VASP's jurisdiction;\n                        in accordance with the targeted financial sanctions regime, conduct AML screening against both the originator and beneficiary;\n                        carry out wallet attribution to establish which type of wallet - custodial or unhosted - is used on the other end and, if it is a custodial one, establish which VASP controls the wallet;\n                        conduct transaction monitoring and report to the FIU if a suspicious transaction is identified;\n                        transmit information about the originator and beneficiary to the beneficiary or intermediary VASP; and\n                        keep records related to the transfer for as long as required by national legislation.\n                                       \n        \n                                        \n                                            Regulation\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Regulation (EU) 2023/1113\n                                                Travel rule Guidelines\n                                                Loi relative à la prévention du blanchiment de capitaux et du financement du terrorisme et à la limitation de l'utilisation des espèces (Loi anti-blanchiment\n                                            \n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Regulatory body\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            \n                                                Autoriteit voor Financiële Diensten en Markten (FSMA)\n                                            \n                                        \n                                                                        \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Threshold\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            No threshold. The Travel Rule applies to all crypto-asset transfers irrespective of value.\n                                        \n                                    \n                                    \n                                        \n                                            Data to be shared\n                                        \n                                        \n                                            Natural Person\n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                a) Full official name;\n                                                \n                                                    b) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number;\n                                                \n                                                c) Address and identification information: i) habitual residence, including country name; ii) official personal document number; iii) customer identification number, or date and place of birth.\n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                d) Full official name;\n                                                \n                                                    e) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number.\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Legal Entity\n                                            Originator:\n                                            \n                                                f) Registered name;\n                                                \n                                                    g) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number;\n                                                \n                                                h) Registered office address;\n                                                \n                                                    i) Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) or equivalent identifier. The alternative identifier must: i) be a unique identification code for the legal entity; ii) be published in public registries; iii) be automatically issued upon entity\n                                                    formation by a public authority; iv) allow identification of name and address elements; v) be accompanied by a description of the identifier type in the messaging system.\n                                                \n                                            \n                                            Beneficiary:\n                                            \n                                                j) Registered name;\n                                                \n                                                    k) Distributed ledger address and crypto-asset account number: If the transfer is registered on a DLT network, include the distributed ledger address and the crypto-asset account number if it exists and is used. If not registered on\n                                                    a DLT network, provide the crypto-asset account number;\n                                                \n                                                \n                                                    l) Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) or equivalent identifier. The alternative identifier must: i) be a unique identification code for the legal entity; ii) be published in public registries; iii) be automatically issued upon entity\n                                                    formation by a public authority; iv) allow identification of name and address elements; v) be accompanied by a description of the identifier type in the messaging system.\n
